title: ScrubConfig
description:
Top-level `scrub.toml` configuration.

Controls the 3-layer scrubbing pipeline: built-in rules, user-defined
rules, and HMAC tokenisation. All scrubbing happens at write time
before any recording hits disk.

rules.description: User-defined scrub rules (applied after built-in rules).

ApplyTarget.description: Restricts where a regex scrub rule is applied.

PiiAction.description:
What to do with a PII match at scrub-write time.

**Not yet enforced** — parsed and stored only. Doctor uses
[`PiiConfig::allowlist`] for suppression and ignores this field.

oneOf.description: Replace with a deterministic HMAC token.
Refuse to write the recording at all (hard fail).

PiiConfig.description:
PII-detection policy section (`[pii]`).

Consulted by `wraith doctor`'s PII audit pass: if `detect = false` the
audit is skipped wholesale; otherwise each candidate finding's location
(a JSON-pointer-ish path like `request.body.user.email` or
`$.routes[0].response_template.author.email`) is checked against
`allowlist`, and matching paths are suppressed.

`default_action` is parsed and round-trip-stable, but is not yet wired
into scrub-on-write — that is a follow-up bone. Doctor never consults
it.

allowlist.description:
JSON-path-ish glob patterns that bypass PII detection. A finding is
suppressed if its location matches any pattern via
[`pii_path_matches`]. Patterns may start with `$` (anchor stripped
before matching) and may contain `*` to match a single path segment.
See [`pii_path_matches`] for the supported grammar.

default_action.description:
Action used when emitting scrubbed PII at write time. Parsed and
preserved for round-trip but not yet enforced — see TODO in
`capture/scrub.rs`. Defaults to [`PiiAction::Tokenize`].

detect.description:
Master toggle. When false, doctor skips the PII audit pass entirely
(other doctor checks still run).

fields.description:
Per-field manual overrides (bn-1p6v). `fields.always` lists JSON paths
that are *always* treated as PII regardless of cardinality detection
or value-shape heuristics. The complement of `allowlist`. Used when
auto-detection misclassifies a real PII field as enum (e.g. a small
fixture where every entity has 'Alice').

PiiFieldsConfig.description:
Per-field manual overrides for PII detection (bn-1p6v).

`always` entries take precedence over both the cardinality-detected enum
set (`RouteModel.enum_paths`) and the static
`capture::pii::DEFAULT_NAME_KEY_ALLOWLIST` — but NOT over [`PiiConfig::allowlist`],
which remains the highest-precedence layer (its semantics from bn-2fdz
are preserved). The chain is documented on
[`crate::capture::pii::scrub_pii_in_json_confident`] and
[`crate::doctor::checks::run_pii_audit`].

TOML shape:
```toml
[pii.fields]
always = [
  "$.author.email",
  "$.viewer.name",
]
```

Patterns use the same `pii_path_matches` grammar as `[pii] allowlist`:
`*` matches one path segment, leading `$` is anchor-stripped, matching
is suffix-based.

always.description: JSON paths that are unconditionally treated as PII. Empty by default.

ScrubAction.description: Action to take when a scrub rule matches.

oneOf.description:
Replace with a stable pseudonym (`user_<base62>`).

Like [`Self::Tokenize`] this is a deterministic HMAC-SHA256 of the
matched value with the recording's HMAC key, but emits a `user_`-prefixed
envelope intended for human-identity replacement (e.g. real GitHub
usernames in URL path segments). Idempotent: applying the action to an
already-pseudonymized value (`user_<base62>`) returns it unchanged so
re-running the scrub pass — at record, synth, export, or pack time —
produces stable output. See bn-280e.

action.description: What to do with matched values.

apply_to.description: Restrict matching to specific locations (default: all).

match.description: Pattern to match (exact name for header/cookie/query, `JSONPath`, or regex).

scope.description: Where to look for the match (header, cookie, jsonpath, regex, query param).

ScrubScope.description: Where a scrub rule looks for matches.

oneOf.description: Match by `JSONPath` expression in request/response bodies.
Match by regex anywhere in the exchange.
Match by URL query parameter name.

TokenizeMode.description: How matched secret values are replaced.

oneOf.description: Replace with a user-supplied mask string.
